name: The Credit Conscious Network
description_short: Marketplace app connecting consumers with vetted credit repair specialists. Free for clients; specialists pay for unlimited access. No commissions taken.
description_long:
The Credit Conscious Network was founded in 2015 as Credit Conscious Network LLC, headquartered in Duluth, Georgia, with a secondary presence in Athens, Georgia — where it holds membership with the Athens Area Chamber of Commerce. Built around the premise that consumers navigating credit challenges deserve better access to qualified professionals, the company created a digital matchmaking platform rather than offering credit repair services directly. No CDFI, HUD-approved, or NFCC certifications have been verified in publicly available sources, and the company does not operate as a nonprofit counseling agency.

The platform functions as a two-sided marketplace: consumers download the free app, browse vetted credit repair specialists in their area, review profiles, and initiate contact — all without paying anything. Beyond credit repair, the platform extends into adjacent financial services, connecting clients with mortgage brokers, realtors, loan officers, insurance providers, and retirement consultants. An in-app educational video library covers credit restoration, business expansion, real estate, and investing fundamentals. The mobile app is available on both iOS (App Store ID: 6450050388) and Android (Google Play), and customer support is accessible by phone, text, or email. An Athens, Georgia location also appears under accounting and debt reduction consulting services through the local Chamber.

The Credit Conscious Network's most notable differentiator is its commission-free model for specialists: professionals keep 100% of what they earn from clients, paying only a subscription fee for platform access (their first client connection is free upon signup). This stands in contrast to lead-generation platforms that take a cut of specialist revenue. Client-controlled matching gives consumers the ability to accept or reject specialist connection requests, shifting discovery power to the consumer rather than the platform. Specialists are screened before being listed, providing a baseline quality filter. The company holds a 5.0/5 Google rating from 43 reviews as of early 2026, reflecting a consistently positive user experience.

The platform's zero-cost entry for consumers and no-commission structure for specialists creates genuine value on both sides of the marketplace. However, because the company itself does not perform credit repair or dispute any credit bureau items directly, consumer outcomes depend entirely on the individual specialist selected. Exact specialist subscription pricing is not publicly listed, making cost comparisons difficult for professionals evaluating the platform. There is no verifiable BBB profile or accreditation, and no third-party certifications (NFCC, HUD, CDFI) have been confirmed. Consumers should independently vet any matched specialist and understand that the platform's role is introduction and facilitation — not service delivery. Consumers who successfully repair their credit often find better rates on installment loans, secured credit cards, and other financial products.
address: 3235 Satellite Blvd NW Building 400 Suite 300, Duluth, GA 30096

tiers.name: Client Access
features: Free to search and browse vetted credit repair specialists
Send and receive messages with specialists via in-app chat
Accept or reject specialist connection requests
Access to financial education video library
Connections to realtors, mortgage brokers, and loan officers
Customer support by phone, text, or email

tiers.name: Specialist Membership
features: First client connection free upon signup
Paid subscription required for unlimited client access in your area
No commission taken — specialists keep 100% of client earnings
Specialists set their own service pricing
Listed in searchable specialist directory after vetting/screening

pros: Completely free for consumers — no cost to search, connect, or message specialists
Mobile app available on both iOS and Android for convenient access
Specialists keep 100% of client earnings — no commission taken by the platform
Client-controlled matching lets consumers accept or reject specialist requests
Specialists are vetted and screened before being listed on the platform
5.0/5 Google rating from 43 reviews as of early 2026
Extends beyond credit repair to connect users with mortgage brokers, realtors, loan officers, and insurance providers
cons: The platform does not perform credit repair itself — actual results depend entirely on the individual specialist chosen
Specialist subscription pricing is not publicly disclosed, making it difficult for professionals to evaluate cost before signing up
No verifiable BBB profile, accreditation, or third-party certifications (NFCC, HUD, CDFI) found in public sources
Relatively small review base (43 Google reviews) limits the depth of independent consumer feedback available
Specialist quality may vary despite vetting — the platform facilitates introductions but does not guarantee outcomes
best_for: Consumers who want to comparison-shop local credit repair specialists without paying any upfront fees
Independent credit repair professionals seeking a commission-free platform to acquire clients
People who need credit repair guidance alongside mortgage, real estate, or financial planning referrals
Consumers who prefer to control the matching process and vet specialists themselves before committing
diagnosis: The Credit Conscious Network is best suited for cost-conscious consumers who want a no-risk way to find and vet local credit repair specialists, and for independent specialists who want client leads without giving up a commission. The primary caveat is that the platform is a facilitator, not a service provider — the quality of credit repair work depends entirely on which specialist the consumer selects, not on the platform itself.
services: Credit repair specialist matching and referral
Specialist vetting and screening before platform listing
In-app messaging between clients and specialists
Client-controlled specialist browsing and connection requests
Financial education video library (credit restoration, investing, business expansion)
Customer support via phone, text, and email
